/assets/archive/1.mp4: Pee Safe teams up with cricketer Smriti Mandhana in this spirited commercial built around the #BeInYourComfortZone campaign. The film celebrates breaking taboos around women's intimate wellness through candid, confidence-led storytelling and bright, energetic framing. A female-first narrative positions Pee Safe as a companion for everyday comfort, strength, and unapologetic self-assurance.

/assets/archive/19.mp4: Vicks brings fast relief to the feed with a punchy YouTube Shorts ad featuring Guneet Sandhu. In under a minute, the vertical cut demonstrates the Roll On and Inhaler's on-the-go comfort with snappy edits and relatable, congestion-era humour. The film is built for thumb-stopping recall and instant, mobile-first brand association.
/assets/archive/2.mp4: Aarohi Loans for Women champions financial independence for Indian women through a warm, aspirational brand film. Following the journeys of women turning big dreams into reality, the film uses intimate, real-world vignettes and an uplifting score to frame Aarohi as a trusted partner. It reframes borrowing as empowerment, not burden.
/assets/archive/22.jpg: A square campaign still composed for the feed, favouring brand clarity and an editorial calm. The frame extends the shoot's visual language into a single, self-contained social image that reads cleanly at thumbnail scale.
/assets/archive/23.mp4: Disney+ Hotstar launched The Night Manager with a mischievous guerrilla promo starring Aditya Roy Kapur surprising unsuspecting hotel guests. Blending scripted comedy with hidden-camera spontaneity, the film amplifies the series' intrigue while showcasing Aditya's charisma. It's a playful, shareable tease engineered to drive streaming sign-ups and social buzz.
/assets/archive/24.mp4: A square Instagram still from a campaign shoot, framed for strong grid impact. The composition leans on clean light and confident styling to carry the brand story in a single, scroll-resistant image that rewards a closer look.
/assets/archive/25.mp4: A square social post still composed for Instagram, distilling a campaign's tone into one polished frame. Restrained and brand-forward, it extends the visual narrative into the feed without competing for attention, letting the product and mood speak.
/assets/archive/26.mp4: Skinn's Noura fragrance is celebrated as a gift from you, to you in this sensorial brand film. Soft, feminine cinematography and a delicate rhythm frame Noura as an act of self-love rather than occasion-wear luxury. The film leans into intimacy and ritual, positioning the scent as a daily, personal indulgence.
/assets/archive/27.mp4: Dell's Futurist initiative teams Alienware with electronic duo Lost Stories to turn cutting-edge tech into music. This brand film follows the artists composing on high-performance machines, merging product capability with creative ambition. Energetic, tech-glossy visuals position Alienware as the instrument for makers pushing what's possible next.
/assets/archive/28.mp4: 'World Is Yours To Take' is a brand film championing sustainability in Indian streetwear. Following young creators reimagining fashion through conscious choices, it pairs documentary warmth with bold, street-styled visuals. The film frames responsible design as cultural ownership, encouraging a generation to wear its values and claim the future on its own terms.
/assets/archive/3.mp4: Centrum enlists actor Kajal Aggarwal as its Chief Claims Advocate in this polished commercial that demystifies daily nutritional gaps. Through clean, credibility-driven direction and a confident on-screen presence, the film communicates Centrum's science-backed multivitamin promise, balancing star appeal with product clarity to earn the trust of health-conscious households.
/assets/archive/4.mp4: A vertical Instagram reel crafted for mobile-first storytelling, pairing tight, energetic framing with a syncopated edit built to stop the scroll. Designed for discovery and shareability, it distils a campaign moment into seconds of punchy, platform-native motion that rewards repeated viewing in the feed.
/assets/archive/5.mp4: Armani Exchange taps actor Kartik Aaryan for its Fall 2024 watch collection in a slick, rhythm-driven fashion film. Shot with editorial precision and a metropolitan mood, the spot fuses product hero shots with Kartik's effortless charm. The result is a youthful, premium statement that frames the watches as essential everyday luxury.
/assets/archive/6.1.jpg: A square collaboration still for Instagram, captured from the set and framed for a clean grid. The image carries an easy, behind-the-scenes mood, pairing the studio's craft with a partner brand in a single, editorial composition that feels spontaneous yet considered.
